title: N003: Add optional Webhook and monitoring templates
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'Helm Chart Packaging

Chart structure under charts/'

Implement all conditionally-rendered Helm templates for optional components. Includes: templates/webhook/ with MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, and webhook Service (gated by webhook.enabled); templates/certmanager/ with Issuer and Certificate resources (gated by certmanager.enabled, which implies webhook.enabled); templates/servicemonitor.yaml for Prometheus monitoring (gated by serviceMonitor.enabled); and templates/networkpolicy.yaml for metrics ingress policy (gated by networkPolicy.enabled). Each template includes proper conditional blocks and cross-references (e.g., cert-manager Certificate references webhook Service).

**Rationale:** These optional components represent the full feature set of the operator deployment. Webhooks and cert-manager are tightly coupled and must be templated together. ServiceMonitor and NetworkPolicy are independent but complete the chart's parity with the existing Kustomize configuration.

**Affected Areas:**
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/webhook/mutating-webhook.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/webhook/validating-webhook.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/webhook/service.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/certmanager/issuer.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/certmanager/certificate.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/servicemonitor.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/networkpolicy.yaml
- config/webhook/
- config/certmanager/
- config/prometheus/

stories.title: Cluster admin can enable admission webhooks via Helm values
stories.want: to deploy MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, and webhook Service by setting webhook.enabled=true
stories.so_that: the operator's defaulting and validation webhooks intercept Memcached CR create/update requests
criteria: Setting webhook.enabled=true renders MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, and webhook Service (3 documents total across webhook/ templates)
Setting webhook.enabled=false renders zero documents from templates/webhook/
Webhook configurations reference the webhook Service by fullname in clientConfig.service.name
Webhook Service targets pods with selectorLabels + control-plane: controller-manager on port 443 -> targetPort 9443
All webhook resources include standard Helm labels from _helpers.tpl
MutatingWebhookConfiguration has correct path /mutate-memcached-c5c3-io-v1beta1-memcached and sideEffects: None
ValidatingWebhookConfiguration has correct path /validate-memcached-c5c3-io-v1beta1-memcached and sideEffects: None

stories.title: Cluster admin can enable cert-manager integration for webhook TLS
stories.want: to deploy a self-signed Issuer and Certificate for the webhook by setting certmanager.enabled=true
stories.so_that: cert-manager automatically provisions and rotates TLS certificates for the webhook server
criteria: Setting certmanager.enabled=true renders Issuer and Certificate resources (2 documents total)
Setting certmanager.enabled=false renders zero documents from templates/certmanager/
Certificate dnsNames reference the webhook Service fullname in the release namespace (SERVICE.NAMESPACE.svc and SERVICE.NAMESPACE.svc.cluster.local)
Certificate issuerRef.name matches the Issuer name
Certificate secretName is webhook-server-cert
Both webhook configurations include cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation referencing NAMESPACE/CERT-NAME when certmanager.enabled=true
Issuer and Certificate include standard Helm labels

stories.title: Cluster admin can enable Prometheus ServiceMonitor
stories.want: to deploy a ServiceMonitor resource by setting serviceMonitor.enabled=true
stories.so_that: Prometheus automatically discovers and scrapes operator metrics
criteria: Setting serviceMonitor.enabled=true renders exactly 1 ServiceMonitor document
Setting serviceMonitor.enabled=false (default) renders zero documents
ServiceMonitor selector matches pods with selectorLabels + control-plane: controller-manager
ServiceMonitor endpoint targets port 'https' (8443) with /metrics path, scheme https, and bearerTokenFile
ServiceMonitor includes standard Helm labels plus control-plane: controller-manager label

stories.title: Cluster admin can customize NetworkPolicy for webhook port access
stories.want: the NetworkPolicy to include webhook port 9443 ingress when webhooks are enabled
stories.so_that: the API server can reach the webhook endpoint through the network policy
criteria: When webhook.enabled=true, NetworkPolicy ingress includes port 9443 in addition to health (8081) and metrics (8443) ports
When webhook.enabled=false, NetworkPolicy ingress does NOT include port 9443
Existing health and metrics port rules remain unchanged regardless of webhook.enabled

stories.title: Cluster admin gets correct Deployment configuration when webhooks are enabled
stories.want: the Deployment to include webhook server port and certificate volume mount when webhook.enabled=true
stories.so_that: the operator manager container can serve webhook requests with TLS
criteria: When webhook.enabled=true, Deployment container ports include port 9443 named webhook-server
When webhook.enabled=true, Deployment container has volumeMount for /tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
When webhook.enabled=true, Deployment pod spec has volume 'cert' referencing secret webhook-server-cert
When webhook.enabled=false, none of the webhook port, volumeMount, or volume are present
Existing ports (health 8081, metrics 8443) remain unchanged

stories.title: Integration: all templates render together without conflicts
stories.want: all templates to render consistently when all features are enabled or selectively disabled
stories.so_that: helm install/upgrade produces valid, interconnected Kubernetes resources
criteria: All defaults (webhook=true, certmanager=true, serviceMonitor=false, networkPolicy=true) render correct total document count
fullnameOverride propagates to all new resource names (webhook service, webhook configs, cert-manager resources, servicemonitor)
Disabling webhook.enabled suppresses all webhook templates, cert-manager templates (since certmanager implies webhook), and removes webhook port from Deployment and NetworkPolicy

Cross-references between templates are consistent: webhook configs reference webhook service name, cert-manager Certificate references webhook service DNS, webhook configs have cert-manager CA injection annotation

stories.title: Cluster admin sees NOTES.txt output reflecting enabled optional components
stories.want: the post-install NOTES to mention which optional components are enabled
stories.so_that: I know the current deployment configuration at a glance
criteria: NOTES.txt shows 'Webhooks: enabled' when webhook.enabled=true, 'Webhooks: disabled' when false
NOTES.txt shows 'cert-manager: enabled' when certmanager.enabled=true
NOTES.txt shows 'ServiceMonitor: enabled' when serviceMonitor.enabled=true

requirements.description: The chart SHALL render MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, and webhook Service when webhook.enabled is true, and SHALL render nothing from templates/webhook/ when false
requirements.rationale: Webhook resources are optional and must be conditionally rendered to support installations without webhook infrastructure

scenarios.name: Webhooks enabled renders 3 resources
scenarios.then: templates/webhook/mutating-webhook.yaml renders 1 MutatingWebhookConfiguration, templates/webhook/validating-webhook.yaml renders 1 ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, templates/webhook/service.yaml renders 1 Service
and_then: all 3 resources include standard Helm labels

scenarios.name: Webhooks disabled renders nothing
scenarios.then: all three webhook templates render 0 documents each

scenarios.name: MutatingWebhookConfiguration has correct webhook path and rules
scenarios.then: MutatingWebhookConfiguration has webhook entry with path /mutate-memcached-c5c3-io-v1beta1-memcached, failurePolicy Fail, sideEffects None, admissionReviewVersions [v1]
and_then: rules target memcached.c5c3.io/v1beta1 memcacheds for CREATE and UPDATE

requirements.description: The webhook Service SHALL select operator pods via selectorLabels + control-plane: controller-manager and expose port 443 targeting 9443
requirements.rationale: The Kubernetes API server sends webhook requests to port 443 of the webhook Service, which must forward to the manager's webhook port 9443

scenarios.name: Service selector matches Deployment pods
scenarios.then: webhook Service selector includes selectorLabels (app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance) plus control-plane: controller-manager

scenarios.name: Service port mapping is correct
scenarios.then: webhook Service has port 443 with targetPort 9443 and protocol TCP

scenarios.name: Service name uses fullname helper
scenarios.then: webhook Service name is custom-webhook-service
and_then: webhook configurations reference this name in clientConfig.service.name

requirements.description: The chart SHALL render cert-manager Issuer and Certificate when certmanager.enabled is true, and SHALL render nothing when false
requirements.rationale: cert-manager integration provides automated TLS certificate management for webhooks; it must be optional as not all clusters have cert-manager

scenarios.name: cert-manager enabled renders Issuer and Certificate
scenarios.then: templates/certmanager/issuer.yaml renders 1 Issuer, templates/certmanager/certificate.yaml renders 1 Certificate
and_then: Issuer is selfSigned, Certificate references the Issuer by name

scenarios.name: cert-manager disabled renders nothing
scenarios.then: both cert-manager templates render 0 documents

scenarios.name: Certificate dnsNames reference webhook Service
scenarios.then: Certificate dnsNames include FULLNAME-webhook-service.NAMESPACE.svc and FULLNAME-webhook-service.NAMESPACE.svc.cluster.local

requirements.description: When certmanager.enabled is true, the MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration SHALL include the cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation
requirements.rationale: cert-manager uses this annotation to inject the CA bundle into webhook configurations so the API server trusts the webhook's TLS certificate

scenarios.name: CA injection annotation present when cert-manager enabled
scenarios.then: both webhook configurations have annotation cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from: NAMESPACE/FULLNAME-serving-cert

scenarios.name: CA injection annotation absent when cert-manager disabled
scenarios.then: webhook configurations do NOT have the cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation

scenarios.name: Annotation value matches Certificate resource
scenarios.when: certmanager.enabled=true in namespace my-ns with fullnameOverride=custom
scenarios.then: annotation value is my-ns/custom-serving-cert matching the Certificate metadata.name

requirements.description: The chart SHALL render a ServiceMonitor when serviceMonitor.enabled is true, targeting the operator's metrics endpoint on port https (8443)
requirements.rationale: ServiceMonitor enables Prometheus service discovery for the operator's metrics; it's disabled by default as not all clusters run Prometheus Operator

scenarios.name: ServiceMonitor enabled renders 1 resource
scenarios.then: templates/servicemonitor.yaml renders 1 ServiceMonitor with apiVersion monitoring.coreos.com/v1
and_then: includes standard Helm labels plus control-plane: controller-manager

scenarios.name: ServiceMonitor disabled renders nothing
scenarios.then: templates/servicemonitor.yaml renders 0 documents

scenarios.name: ServiceMonitor endpoint configuration is correct
scenarios.then: endpoint has path /metrics, port https, scheme https, bearerTokenFile /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token, tlsConfig.insecureSkipVerify true
and_then: selector matchLabels includes selectorLabels + control-plane: controller-manager

requirements.description: The NetworkPolicy SHALL conditionally include webhook port 9443 in ingress rules when webhook.enabled is true
requirements.rationale: When webhooks are enabled, the API server must be able to reach port 9443 through the NetworkPolicy

scenarios.name: Webhook port included when webhooks enabled
scenarios.then: NetworkPolicy ingress rules include port 9443 TCP in addition to ports 8081 and 8443

scenarios.name: Webhook port excluded when webhooks disabled
scenarios.then: NetworkPolicy ingress rules include only ports 8081 and 8443, NOT 9443

scenarios.name: NetworkPolicy disabled renders nothing regardless
scenarios.then: networkpolicy.yaml renders 0 documents

requirements.description: The Deployment SHALL conditionally include webhook port, volume mount, and cert volume when webhook.enabled is true
requirements.rationale: The operator manager needs the webhook port exposed and TLS certs mounted to serve admission webhook requests

scenarios.name: Webhook port and volumes added when enabled
scenarios.then: Deployment container ports include containerPort 9443 named webhook-server, container has volumeMount at /tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs, pod has volume 'cert' from secret webhook-server-cert

scenarios.name: No webhook artifacts when disabled
scenarios.then: Deployment container ports do NOT include 9443, no serving-certs volumeMount, no cert volume

scenarios.name: Existing ports unchanged
scenarios.then: Deployment always includes health port 8081 and metrics port 8443

requirements.description: The webhook configurations SHALL reference the webhook Service via clientConfig.service using the fullname-derived service name and the release namespace
requirements.rationale: Webhook configurations must correctly reference the Service so the API server can route admission requests

scenarios.name: Default service reference
scenarios.then: clientConfig.service.name is FULLNAME-webhook-service and clientConfig.service.namespace is .Release.Namespace

scenarios.name: fullnameOverride propagates to service reference
scenarios.then: clientConfig.service.name is custom-webhook-service

scenarios.name: Both webhook configs use same service reference
scenarios.then: MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration both reference the same service name and namespace

requirements.description: The NOTES.txt SHOULD display status of optional components (webhooks, cert-manager, ServiceMonitor)
requirements.rationale: Post-install notes help the admin verify which optional features are active in the deployment

scenarios.name: All enabled shows all components
scenarios.then: NOTES output includes lines indicating webhooks enabled, cert-manager enabled, ServiceMonitor enabled

scenarios.name: Webhooks without cert-manager shows warning
scenarios.then: NOTES output includes warning that webhook TLS certificates must be managed manually

scenarios.name: Webhooks disabled shows disabled status
scenarios.then: NOTES output indicates webhooks are disabled

tasks.title: Create webhook Service template (REQ-002)
tasks.description: Create `charts/memcached-operator/templates/webhook/service.yaml`. Gated by `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}`. Translates `config/webhook/service.yaml` into Helm template: name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-webhook-service`, namespace `{{ .Release.Namespace }}`, standard labels from `_helpers.tpl`, port 443 -> targetPort 9443, selector using `selectorLabels` + `control-plane: controller-manager`. Create test file `charts/memcached-operator/tests/webhook_service_test.yaml` with tests for: renders 1 doc when enabled, renders 0 when disabled, correct name/namespace/labels/port/selector, fullnameOverride propagation.

tasks.title: Create MutatingWebhookConfiguration template (REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create `charts/memcached-operator/templates/webhook/mutating-webhook.yaml`. Gated by `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}`. Translates `config/webhook/manifests.yaml` (mutating section): name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-mutating-webhook-configuration`, standard labels, clientConfig.service.name referencing the webhook service (`{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-webhook-service`), clientConfig.service.namespace `{{ .Release.Namespace }}`, path `/mutate-memcached-c5c3-io-v1beta1-memcached`, failurePolicy Fail, sideEffects None, admissionReviewVersions [v1], rules for memcached.c5c3.io/v1beta1 CREATE+UPDATE. Conditionally add `cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from` annotation when `certmanager.enabled=true`. Create test file `charts/memcached-operator/tests/webhook_mutating_test.yaml`.

tasks.title: Create ValidatingWebhookConfiguration template (REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create `charts/memcached-operator/templates/webhook/validating-webhook.yaml`. Same pattern as task 1.2 but for validating webhook: name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-validating-webhook-configuration`, path `/validate-memcached-c5c3-io-v1beta1-memcached`, webhook name `vmemcached-v1beta1.kb.io`. Same conditional cert-manager annotation. Create test file `charts/memcached-operator/tests/webhook_validating_test.yaml`.

tasks.title: Create cert-manager Issuer template (REQ-003)
tasks.description: Create `charts/memcached-operator/templates/certmanager/issuer.yaml`. Gated by `{{- if .Values.certmanager.enabled }}`. Translates `config/certmanager/certificate.yaml` (Issuer section): apiVersion cert-manager.io/v1, kind Issuer, name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-selfsigned-issuer`, namespace `{{ .Release.Namespace }}`, standard labels, spec.selfSigned {}. Create test file `charts/memcached-operator/tests/certmanager_issuer_test.yaml` with tests for: renders when enabled, doesn't render when disabled, correct name/namespace/labels, selfSigned spec.

tasks.title: Create cert-manager Certificate template (REQ-003)
tasks.description: Create `charts/memcached-operator/templates/certmanager/certificate.yaml`. Gated by `{{- if .Values.certmanager.enabled }}`. Translates `config/certmanager/certificate.yaml` (Certificate section): apiVersion cert-manager.io/v1, kind Certificate, name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-serving-cert`, namespace `{{ .Release.Namespace }}`, standard labels, dnsNames using webhook service fullname (`{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-webhook-service.{{ .Release.Namespace }}.svc` and `.svc.cluster.local`), issuerRef.name referencing the Issuer (`{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-selfsigned-issuer`), issuerRef.kind Issuer, secretName webhook-server-cert, privateKey.rotationPolicy Always. Create test file `charts/memcached-operator/tests/certmanager_certificate_test.yaml`.

tasks.title: Create ServiceMonitor template (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Create `charts/memcached-operator/templates/servicemonitor.yaml`. Gated by `{{- if .Values.serviceMonitor.enabled }}`. Translates `config/prometheus/monitor.yaml`: apiVersion monitoring.coreos.com/v1, kind ServiceMonitor, name `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-metrics-monitor`, namespace `{{ .Release.Namespace }}`, standard labels + `control-plane: controller-manager`, endpoint with path /metrics, port https, scheme https, bearerTokenFile, tlsConfig.insecureSkipVerify true, selector.matchLabels using selectorLabels + control-plane: controller-manager. Create test file `charts/memcached-operator/tests/servicemonitor_test.yaml`.

tasks.title: Update Deployment template for conditional webhook port/volumes (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Modify `charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` to conditionally add webhook artifacts when `webhook.enabled=true`: (1) Add containerPort 9443 named webhook-server to container ports, (2) Add volumeMount for /tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs with name cert and readOnly true, (3) Add volume 'cert' from secret webhook-server-cert with defaultMode 420. All three gated by `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}`. Pattern follows `config/default/manager_webhook_patch.yaml`. Update `charts/memcached-operator/tests/deployment_test.yaml` with new test cases for webhook port/volume presence when enabled and absence when disabled.

tasks.title: Update NetworkPolicy template for conditional webhook port (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Modify `charts/memcached-operator/templates/networkpolicy.yaml` to conditionally add port 9443 TCP to ingress rules when `webhook.enabled=true`. Add `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}` block for the additional port entry. Update existing tests in a new or existing test file to verify: port 9443 present when webhook.enabled=true, absent when false, other ports unchanged.

tasks.title: Update NOTES.txt with optional component status (REQ-009)
tasks.description: Modify `charts/memcached-operator/templates/NOTES.txt` to add sections for: (1) Webhooks status (enabled/disabled), (2) cert-manager status with warning if webhook.enabled=true but certmanager.enabled=false about manual TLS cert management, (3) ServiceMonitor status. Use `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}` etc. conditionals. Update `charts/memcached-operator/tests/helpers_test.yaml` (NOTES.txt suite) with tests for NOTES output under different value combinations.

tasks.title: Update integration tests for new templates (REQ-001 through REQ-008)
tasks.description: Update `charts/memcached-operator/tests/integration_test.yaml` to: (1) Add all new templates to the templates list (webhook/mutating-webhook.yaml, webhook/validating-webhook.yaml, webhook/service.yaml, certmanager/issuer.yaml, certmanager/certificate.yaml, servicemonitor.yaml), (2) Update default resource count test to include webhook (3), certmanager (2), servicemonitor (0) documents, (3) Add test for webhook.enabled=false suppressing all webhook + certmanager templates, (4) Add test for certmanager.enabled=false suppressing certmanager templates while webhook templates remain, (5) Add test for serviceMonitor.enabled=true producing 1 ServiceMonitor, (6) Add fullnameOverride propagation tests for new resources, (7) Verify cross-references: webhook configs reference correct service name, cert annotation references correct Certificate name.

tasks.title: Add _helpers.tpl webhook service name helper
tasks.description: Add a helper `memcached-operator.webhookServiceName` to `charts/memcached-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` that returns `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-webhook-service`. This centralizes the webhook service name used by webhook/service.yaml, webhook configurations clientConfig, and Certificate dnsNames. Add a helper `memcached-operator.servingCertName` that returns `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-serving-cert` for the Certificate name and the cert-manager annotation. Update _helpers.tpl tests to verify these new helpers.

review_criteria: All webhook templates (mutating-webhook.yaml, validating-webhook.yaml, service.yaml) render correctly when webhook.enabled=true and produce 0 documents when false

cert-manager templates (issuer.yaml, certificate.yaml) render correctly when certmanager.enabled=true and produce 0 documents when false; Certificate dnsNames correctly reference webhook service fullname
ServiceMonitor template renders when serviceMonitor.enabled=true with correct endpoint config (port https, path /metrics, scheme https, bearerTokenFile) and produces 0 documents when false
NetworkPolicy conditionally includes port 9443 only when webhook.enabled=true; existing ports 8081 and 8443 remain unconditional
Deployment conditionally includes webhook port 9443, cert volumeMount, and cert volume only when webhook.enabled=true; existing container config unchanged
cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation conditionally present on both webhook configurations only when certmanager.enabled=true, with correct NAMESPACE/CERT-NAME value
fullnameOverride propagates correctly to all new resource names and cross-references (webhook service name in clientConfig, Certificate dnsNames, cert-manager annotation value)

All helm-unittest test files pass: webhook_service_test, webhook_mutating_test, webhook_validating_test, certmanager_issuer_test, certmanager_certificate_test, servicemonitor_test, updated deployment_test, updated integration_test
NOTES.txt displays correct status for webhooks, cert-manager, and ServiceMonitor components
All new templates follow existing patterns: {{- if .Values.X.enabled }} guard, standard labels via _helpers.tpl, namespace from .Release.Namespace, names from fullname helper
implementation_notes:
All templates translate existing Kustomize manifests from config/ into Helm templates. Key patterns:

1. **Naming convention**: All new resources use `{{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-SUFFIX` pattern (e.g., -webhook-service, -mutating-webhook-configuration, -selfsigned-issuer, -serving-cert, -metrics-monitor).

2. **Helper functions**: Add `memcached-operator.webhookServiceName` and `memcached-operator.servingCertName` to _helpers.tpl to centralize names used across multiple templates.

3. **Conditional rendering**: Each template file starts with `{{- if .Values.COMPONENT.enabled }}` and ends with `{{- end }}`. The cert-manager annotation on webhook configs uses a nested conditional.

4. **Cross-references**: Webhook configs reference webhook Service by helper name. Certificate dnsNames reference webhook Service fullname + namespace. cert-manager annotation references namespace + Certificate name. These must stay consistent under fullnameOverride.

5. **NetworkPolicy update**: Add conditional port 9443 inside the existing ingress ports list. Use `{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}` around just the additional port entry.

6. **Deployment update**: Follow the pattern from config/default/manager_webhook_patch.yaml — add webhook port, volumeMount, and volume conditionally.

7. **Test conventions**: Follow existing helm-unittest YAML format with suite/templates/release/tests structure. Each test file covers: gate test (enabled/disabled), kind/apiVersion, name/namespace/labels, specific fields, fullnameOverride propagation.

8. **Reference configs**: config/webhook/manifests.yaml (webhook configs), config/webhook/service.yaml (webhook service), config/certmanager/certificate.yaml (issuer + certificate), config/prometheus/monitor.yaml (servicemonitor), config/network-policy/allow-metrics-traffic.yaml (networkpolicy reference), config/default/manager_webhook_patch.yaml (deployment webhook patch).
